notes: Generic chocolate wafer - too many similar products already exist in US (Kit Kat, Pirouline, etc). No strong differentiation angle.
Premium nut chocolate bars already saturated in US - see Lindt, Ghirardelli, Tony's Chocolonely, hundreds of craft brands. Hard to differentiate.
Chip category has razor-thin margins and massive competition. Even with exotic flavors, hard to compete against Frito-Lay distribution and pricing power.
Candy bar market is hyper-competitive. Hard to get shelf space and margins are poor unless you have massive scale. Similar to existing protein/granola bars.
Shrimp crackers work in Asian contexts but haven't crossed over to mainstream US despite being available for decades. Limited addressable market.
Chocolate wafer rolls already exist (Pirouline). No unique angle beyond being 'premium' which is hard to defend without massive investment.
Coconut cookies already well-established in US (Girl Scout Samoas, dozens of keto coconut cookies). Hard to differentiate.
Cookie tins are commodity products. Danish butter cookie tins already dominate this space. No clear differentiation possible.
Butter cookie market saturated - see Walker's Shortbread, Tate's, Danish butter cookies. Would need massive marketing budget to break through.
Herbal supplements with health claims face FDA scrutiny. Complex regulatory path for traditional medicine ingredients not approved in US.
Another chocolate wafer - same issue as Tango and Astor. Market already has Kit Kat, Twix, dozens of wafer bars. No unique angle.
Flavored coated nuts already dominated by established brands (Blue Diamond, Emerald). Bar snack space is crowded and low-margin.
Gummy candy market extremely competitive. Tropical flavors already exist from Haribo, Albanese, and dozens of craft brands. Hard to differentiate.
